[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2025-08-21
[TITLE]: The M84 Machine Gun in the Serbian Army | thefirearmblog.com
[METADATA/INTRO]: The M84 Machine Gun in the Serbian Army Machine guns have long served as the backbone of modern infantry units across the globe, and the Serbian Army is no exception. Researching the history of the Yugoslavian small arms, I discovered...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: The handful of training videos I found showed soldiers using the M84 on a tripod and, in some cases, with an optic, which was a first for me. Typically, nations that use PKMs never use a tripod. When placed on a tripod, it would provide stability and accuracy at greater ranges than the bipod, allowing for closer use in advancing formations. The tripod for the M84 is a Serbian-produced version of the Russian Stepanov 6T5 tripod, lacking a proper traverse and elevation mechanism for fine adjustments. However, it is equipped with coarse adjustments. 
 As someone who gravitates to belt-fed machine guns, particularly the PKM, it is one of the best general-purpose machine guns currently in service. What I find fascinating about Serbia is that the M84 fulfills the role of both a squad automatic weapon and a medium machine gun, providing a cost-effective and logistically efficient solution. This dual-role capability simplifies training requirements, streamlines supply chains, and enhances operational flexibility without sacrificing firepower.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2022-02-24
[TITLE]: Russia: Ready for the worst: Life in the trenches of Ukraine’s Donbas region | International | EL PAÍS English
[METADATA/INTRO]: Ready for the worst: Life in the trenches of Ukraine’s Donbas region EL PAÍS visits the frontline of the eight-year conflict between the Ukrainian army and Russian-backed separatists a day before Moscow’s full-blown attack...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Standing under the camouflage nets that cover the roof of one of the observation posts, Oleg, 44, picks up a heavy automatic machine gun as he gets ready for his shift. “This is always dangerous, it’s war, but there’s no choice,” he says. “We are home, in our land.”
